findings.description: The title and amendment heading in the PDF use blue/dark blue colored text, while the HTML renders them in black. The large adoption and certification text in the PDF is rendered in a much larger display font compared to the HTML which uses smaller regular text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows 'Town of Manteo Core CAMA Land Use Plan Update 2007' and 'Amendment #1' in blue text with large display-size adoption/certification lines. HTML shows all text in black with adoption/certification lines in normal body text size.

findings.description: The PDF presents the adoption and certification dates as large, prominent display text centered on the page, while the HTML renders them as small left-aligned body text, reducing their visual prominence.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Adopted by the Manteo Town Council on December 3, 2008' and 'Certified by the Coastal Resources Commission on February 12, 2009' are in very large font, centered. HTML: same text is in small body text, left-aligned.

findings.description: The HTML contains a nested <main> element inside another <main> element, which is a structural duplication of the container tag, though it does not duplicate content.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> wrapping another <main>, which is redundant but does not affect content fidelity.
